features.details: Search the automation codebase for all occurrences of '.head' attribute access on PullRequest objects. Identify the exact location in the code where this error occurs and determine if it's a PyGithub API misuse, incorrect object type, or missing null check.

Examine issue #118 to understand what type of processing was being attempted. This will help identify if the issue had unique characteristics (e.g., draft PR, merged PR, or cross-repo PR) that caused the 'head' attribute to be unavailable.

Implement the fix for the 'head' attribute error. This may involve: using the correct PyGithub property name (possibly 'head' needs to be accessed via a different path like 'pull_request.head.ref'), adding null/None checks, or handling the case where the PR object is not fully populated.

Add try-except blocks around PullRequest attribute access to gracefully handle cases where attributes may not be available. Log meaningful error messages that help with debugging while allowing the automation to continue or fail gracefully.

features.description: Add unit tests for PullRequest handling
features.details: Create unit tests that cover various PullRequest states: normal PR, draft PR, merged PR, cross-repository PR, and PRs with missing data. Mock the GitHub API responses to ensure the code handles all edge cases correctly.

Search the entire automation codebase for similar direct attribute access patterns on GitHub objects (Issue, PullRequest, Repository, etc.) that might cause similar AttributeErrors. Add appropriate safeguards.

features.description: Re-process issue #118 and close this issue
features.details: After the fix is deployed, manually re-trigger the automation for issue #118 to verify the fix works. If successful, close issue #125 and ensure issue #118 is properly processed.

overall_approach: 1) Investigate the automation code to locate where the 'head' attribute is being accessed on a PullRequest object, 2) Determine if this is a library API misuse or incorrect object type, 3) Implement the fix with proper error handling, 4) Add tests to prevent regression, and 5) Verify the fix resolves the original issue #118 processing.
potential_risks: The fix may require changes to core automation logic that affects other workflows
Issue #118 may have unique characteristics that triggered this edge case
The PullRequest object structure may vary depending on the GitHub API version or library used
Other similar attribute access patterns may exist elsewhere in the codebase
